864495211f1457303845df646b4d35329a668b81.title: Independent sampling
864495211f1457303845df646b4d35329a668b81.body:
The observed data units
\[
O_1,\ldots,O_n
\]
are independent and identically distributed draws from \(P\).

43969e6df2d5a658b3bbe590c3221dd3266cc01d.title: Margin condition
43969e6df2d5a658b3bbe590c3221dd3266cc01d.body:
For every \(u\) with \(0<u\le u_0\),
\[
P\{0<|\tau_P(X)|\le u\}\le C_m u^\alpha .
\]

8d6c74161dd2a2159207337cf5db08e9346010cc.title: Zero-effect agreement
8d6c74161dd2a2159207337cf5db08e9346010cc.body:
Either
\[
P_X\{\tau_P(X)=0\}=0,
\]
or every policy \(\pi\in\Pi\) agrees with the oracle policy \(\pi^\star_P\) \(P_X\)-almost everywhere on the set \(\{\tau_P(X)=0\}\).

649ce32d95e06353710832155cba330569dd5bfd.title: Overlap decay
649ce32d95e06353710832155cba330569dd5bfd.body:
For all \(u>0\) and \(v>0\) satisfying
\[
v\le c_o u^\gamma,
\]
the joint small-contrast and weak-overlap probability obeys
\[
P\{p_P(X)\le v,\ 0<|\tau_P(X)|\le u\}
   \le C_o u^\alpha v^{1/\gamma},
\]
with the convention that \(v^{1/\gamma}=1\) when \(\gamma=0\).

eeb97450bdb641a90e2ce1c48a6ee7af3ed0ae8c.title: Finite-VC policy class
eeb97450bdb641a90e2ce1c48a6ee7af3ed0ae8c.body:
The policy class \(\Pi\) is a pointwise measurable class of measurable deterministic policies \(\pi:\mathcal X\to\{0,1\}\) with finite VC dimension \(d_\Pi<\infty\).  There exists a countable subclass
\[
\Pi_0=\{\pi_j:j\ge 1\}\subset\Pi
\]
such that every \(\pi\in\Pi\) is the pointwise limit of a sequence of policies from \(\Pi_0\).

248438a3be00f98a32932d68703748a599804c43.title: Oracle policy inclusion
248438a3be00f98a32932d68703748a599804c43.body: For every law \(P\) in the law class, the oracle policy \(\pi^\star_P\) belongs to \(\Pi\).

3a21493fa75600d7ea745441c1c7957a55032404.title: Localized VC envelope
3a21493fa75600d7ea745441c1c7957a55032404.body:
For the pointwise measurable finite-VC policy class \(\Pi\), every centered policy-indexed process with envelope \(B\) and conditional second moment bounded by
\[
C B^2 P_X(D_\pi)
\]
has fixed-radius localized supremum bounded, under margin localization, by
\[
C B n^{-1/2} r^{\alpha/(2+2\alpha)}(\log n)^p .
\]

b262e82d5651f0207dee358e63240d751e91f89b.title: Cross-fitted nuisance rates
b262e82d5651f0207dee358e63240d751e91f89b.body:
The cross-fitted nuisance estimators satisfy, for each \(a\in\{0,1\}\),
\[
\|\widehat\mu_a-\mu_a\|_{L_2(P)}\le r_{\mu,n},
\qquad
\|\widehat e-e\|_{L_2(P)}\le r_{e,n},
\]
and their product rate satisfies
\[
r_{\mu,n}r_{e,n}=O(n^{-1/2}).
\]

acea4bc963307f61a414ef94b5959fc3d2fc09a7.title: Strict-overlap endpoint
acea4bc963307f61a414ef94b5959fc3d2fc09a7.body:
When \(\gamma=0\), there exists a constant \(\underline p\in(0,1/2]\) such that
\[
p_P(X)\ge \underline p
\]
holds \(P_X\)-almost surely.

c9e9ba8cf97b2bd121e5e2537e0e68921a036feb.title: Bounded Cross-Fit Nuisances
c9e9ba8cf97b2bd121e5e2537e0e68921a036feb.body: The cross-fitted outcome-regression estimators \(\widehat\mu_0\) and \(\widehat\mu_1\) take values in \([-1,1]\).

971c6d5b7a353c5ca61720d3388acc6fcef8ed50.title: Polynomial Nuisance Rates
971c6d5b7a353c5ca61720d3388acc6fcef8ed50.body:
There exist exponents \(a\ge 0\) and \(c\ge 1/2\), and constants \(C_\mu\) and \(C_{\mathrm{prod}}\), such that, for all sufficiently large \(n\),
\[
r_{\mu,n}\le C_\mu n^{-a},
\qquad
r_{\mu,n}r_{e,n}\le C_{\mathrm{prod}}n^{-c}.
\]

55209200b21f48fd836ff4d9c33ebda43a227d8e.title: Fixed Cross-Fit Fold Count
55209200b21f48fd836ff4d9c33ebda43a227d8e.body: The number \(K\) of cross-fitting folds is a fixed finite integer independent of \(n\), and the deterministic partition \(I_1,\ldots,I_K\) of the observation indices is balanced.

324133362931254649fad0f8bf0d219fba3af88e.title: Localized Offset Envelope
324133362931254649fad0f8bf0d219fba3af88e.body:
For the pointwise measurable finite-VC policy class \(\Pi\), every centered policy-indexed empirical process
\[
z_\pi=(P_n-P)g_\pi
\]
built from an i.i.d. sample of size \(n\), with envelope \(B\) and conditional second moment bounded by
\[
C B^2 P_X(D_\pi),
\]
satisfies
\[
\mathbb E\sup_{\pi\in\Pi}
\left\{2|z_\pi|-\frac{R_P(\pi)}{4}\right\}_+
\le
C\left(\frac{B^2}{n}\right)^{A_\alpha}(\log n)^p .
\]

b76bdb70ae4299b0e519a30557db7793236b51e1.title: Welfare \(V_P(\pi)\) and Regret \(R_P(\pi)\)
b76bdb70ae4299b0e519a30557db7793236b51e1.body:
For a policy \(\pi\), its welfare under \(P\) is
\[
V_P(\pi)=\mathbb E_P\!\left[\pi(X)\tau_P(X)\right].
\]
The oracle policy is
\[
\pi^\star_P(x)=\mathbf 1\{\tau_P(x)\ge 0\}.
\]
The regret of \(\pi\) under \(P\) is
\[
R_P(\pi)=V_P(\pi^\star_P)-V_P(\pi).
\]

3693601a4f517818b481748078854c89b3a0c6a1.title: Disagreement Set \(D_\pi\)
3693601a4f517818b481748078854c89b3a0c6a1.body:
For a policy \(\pi\), its disagreement set is
\[
D_\pi=\{x\in\mathcal X:\pi(x)\ne \pi^\star_P(x)\}.
\]

8913a8aec560deb1722ef05c1de52dc56faec160.title: Exponents $\beta_{\alpha,\gamma}$, $D_{\alpha,\gamma}$, and $r_\star(\alpha,\gamma)$
8913a8aec560deb1722ef05c1de52dc56faec160.body:
The overlap calibration exponent, denominator exponent, and minimax exponent are
\[
\beta_{\alpha,\gamma}
=
\begin{cases}
0, & \gamma=0,\\
\dfrac{\alpha\gamma}{\alpha+1}, & \gamma>0,
\end{cases}
\qquad
D_{\alpha,\gamma}=2+\alpha+\beta_{\alpha,\gamma},
\qquad
r_\star(\alpha,\gamma)=\frac{1+\alpha}{D_{\alpha,\gamma}}.
\]

2f7d2cead19ab8119b1416eb91e5c03d11d067c1.title: Feasible upper exponent $r_{\mathrm{up}}=r_{\mathrm{feas}}$
2f7d2cead19ab8119b1416eb91e5c03d11d067c1.body:
Fix one nuisance regime \((a,c,C_\mu,C_{\mathrm{prod}})\), and set
\[
A_\alpha=\frac{1+\alpha}{2+\alpha}.
\]

If \(\gamma>0\), fix constants
\[
\bar u\in(0,u_0],
\qquad
q_0\in\bigl(0,\min\{1/2,c_o\bar u^\gamma\}\bigr].
\]
For \(0\le s\le 1/2\) and \(0\le t\le s/\gamma\), define
\[
\phi(s,t)
=
\min\left\{
A_\alpha(1-2s),\,
c-s,\,
a+\frac{s}{2\gamma}+\frac{\alpha t}{2},\,
2a-t
\right\}.
\]
Let \((s_{\mathrm{feas}},t_{\mathrm{feas}})\) be any maximizer of \(\phi\) over this compact feasible set, and set
\[
g_{\mathrm{joint}}(\alpha,\gamma,a,c)
=
\phi(s_{\mathrm{feas}},t_{\mathrm{feas}}),
\qquad
q_n=q_0 n^{-s_{\mathrm{feas}}},
\qquad
u_n=\bar u n^{-t_{\mathrm{feas}}}.
\]
Then \(q_n\le c_o u_n^\gamma\) for all sufficiently large \(n\), and the solved conditional feasible upper exponent is
\[
r_{\mathrm{up}}
=
r_{\mathrm{feas}}
=
\min\left\{r_\star(\alpha,\gamma),\,g_{\mathrm{joint}}(\alpha,\gamma,a,c)\right\}.
\]

If \(\gamma=0\), take a fixed clipping sequence \(q_n=q_0\) with \(q_0\le \underline p/2\), and set
\[
r_{\mathrm{up}}
=
r_{\mathrm{feas}}
=
\min\{A_\alpha,c\}.
\]

25623af7af11c87dff699a8243ddf7b9abdc6645.title: Clipped propensity $e_q(x;\eta)$
25623af7af11c87dff699a8243ddf7b9abdc6645.body:
For a nuisance triple \(\eta=(\mu_0,\mu_1,e)\) and a clipping level \(q\in(0,1/2]\), the clipped propensity score is
\[
e_q(x;\eta)
=
\min\{1-q,\max\{q,e(x)\}\}.
\]

fec97ec6b62183dd2648b5b83b2b253f4a8a0b61.title: Clipped AIPW score $\Gamma_q(O;\eta)$
fec97ec6b62183dd2648b5b83b2b253f4a8a0b61.body:
For \(O=(X,A,Y)\), nuisance triple \(\eta=(\mu_0,\mu_1,e)\), and clipping level \(q\in(0,1/2]\), the clipped augmented inverse-propensity-weighted score for the treatment contrast is
\[
\Gamma_q(O;\eta)
=
\mu_1(X)-\mu_0(X)
+
\frac{A}{e_q(X;\eta)}\{Y-\mu_1(X)\}
-
\frac{1-A}{1-e_q(X;\eta)}\{Y-\mu_0(X)\}.
\]

4c1119426f049d876d97c5b149c64993aaa6ccfe.title: Feasible clipped-AIPW ERM $\widehat\pi_n$
4c1119426f049d876d97c5b149c64993aaa6ccfe.body:
Fix the countable pointwise dense subclass
\[
\Pi_0=\{\pi_j:j\ge 1\}
\]
of \(\Pi\), and fix a deterministic balanced \(K\)-fold partition
\[
I_1,\ldots,I_K
\]
of \(\{1,\ldots,n\}\), with each fold having size \(\lfloor n/K\rfloor\) or \(\lceil n/K\rceil\). Let \(k(i)\) denote the evaluation fold containing observation \(i\). Given foldwise cross-fitted nuisance estimators \(\widehat\eta^{(-k)}\) and a clipping level \(q\in(0,1/2]\), define the empirical welfare criterion
\[
\widehat V_{n,q}(\pi)
=
\frac1n\sum_{i=1}^n
\pi(X_i)\,
\Gamma_q\!\left(O_i;\widehat\eta^{(-k(i))}\right).
\]
Let \(j_n\) be the smallest index \(j\ge1\) such that
\[
\widehat V_{n,q}(\pi_j)
\ge
\sup_{\ell\ge1}\widehat V_{n,q}(\pi_\ell)-\frac1n,
\]
and set
\[
\widehat\pi_n=\pi_{j_n}.
\]
This measurable \(\Pi\)-valued \(1/n\)-empirical risk maximizer over \(\Pi_0\) is the feasible clipped-AIPW ERM. The clipping level \(q\) is the tuning parameter chosen by the bias-variance balance.

0f584d98735172ee3ffe019c931fd8f2516e8dc4.title: Minimax regret $M_n(\alpha,\gamma)$
0f584d98735172ee3ffe019c931fd8f2516e8dc4.body:
The minimax regret over the law class \(\mathcal P_{\alpha,\gamma}\) is
\[
M_n(\alpha,\gamma)
=
\inf_{\widehat\pi_n}
\sup_{P\in\mathcal P_{\alpha,\gamma}}
\mathbb E_P\!\left[
R_P(\widehat\pi_n)
\right],
\]
where the infimum ranges over all measurable data-dependent estimators \(\widehat\pi_n\) taking values in \(\Pi\).

18bf1e9d89f52fc4c234f7611d8873dddf3390bb.title: Conditional upper risk \(U_n(\alpha,\gamma,a,c;\widehat\eta)\)
18bf1e9d89f52fc4c234f7611d8873dddf3390bb.body:
Fix a nuisance regime \((a,c,C_\mu,C_{\mathrm{prod}})\) and the associated deterministic schedules \((q_n,u_n)\). The conditional upper risk is
\[
U_n(\alpha,\gamma,a,c;\widehat\eta)
=
\sup E_P\!\left[R_P(\widehat\pi_n)\right],
\]
where the supremum is over all laws \(P\in\mathcal P_{\alpha,\gamma}\) for which the oracle policy belongs to \(\Pi\), all supplied cross-fitted nuisance estimators \(\widehat\eta^{(-k)}\) satisfying the stated \(L_2(P)\) nuisance-rate bounds, boundedness conditions, and polynomial nuisance-rate bounds with exponents \(a,c\) and constants \(C_\mu,C_{\mathrm{prod}}\), all policy classes \(\Pi\) satisfying the stated finite-VC, localized-envelope, and localized-offset-envelope conditions, and all cross-fitting schemes with fixed fold count \(K\).

da4cef9fd76482596cabe5e1acb07e9ee623c1d1.title: Law class \(\mathcal P_{\alpha,\gamma}\)
da4cef9fd76482596cabe5e1acb07e9ee623c1d1.body: The class \(\mathcal P_{\alpha,\gamma}\) consists of all observed laws \(P\) on \(\Omega\) for which Assumptions~\ref{obj:ass:bounded-outcome}, \ref{obj:ass:positivity}, \ref{obj:ass:margin}, \ref{obj:ass:zero-effect}, \ref{obj:ass:overlap-decay}, and \ref{obj:ass:strict-overlap-endpoint} hold at exponents \((\alpha,\gamma)\).

cd04523827add6a40a9c626d99b93ede094ce08d.title: Two-point witness laws \(P_{n,\sigma}\)
cd04523827add6a40a9c626d99b93ede094ce08d.body:
Let \(\mathcal X=[0,1]\) and let \(P_X\) be Lebesgue measure. Set
\[
h_n=n^{-1/(2+\alpha+\beta_{\alpha,\gamma})},
\qquad
q_n=
\begin{cases}
1/4, & \beta_{\alpha,\gamma}=0,\\
h_n^{\beta_{\alpha,\gamma}}, & \beta_{\alpha,\gamma}>0,
\end{cases}
\]
and
\[
B_n=[0,c_Bh_n^\alpha],
\qquad
P_X(B_n)=c_Bh_n^\alpha .
\]
Choose \(\tau_0\in(u_0,2)\). For each \(\sigma\in\{+,-\}\), the law \(P_{n,\sigma}\) has the same covariate law \(P_X\), the same logging propensity
\[
e(x)=
\begin{cases}
q_n, & x\in B_n,\\
1/2, & x\notin B_n,
\end{cases}
\]
and the same off-block outcome law supported on \(\{-1,1\}\), with
\[
E[Y\mid X=x,A=1]=\tau_0/2,
\qquad
E[Y\mid X=x,A=0]=-\tau_0/2,
\qquad x\notin B_n .
\]
Thus the common off-block optimal label is \(1\), and the common off-block contrast is \(\tau_0\). On \(B_n\), set \(Y=0\) almost surely when \(A=0\). On the active treated cell \(\{X\in B_n,A=1\}\), let \(Y\) be supported on \(\{-1,1\}\) with
\[
P(Y=1\mid X\in B_n,A=1)=\frac{1+\sigma h_n}{2},
\qquad
P(Y=-1\mid X\in B_n,A=1)=\frac{1-\sigma h_n}{2}.
\]
Consequently,
\[
E[Y\mid X\in B_n,A=1]=\sigma h_n,
\qquad
\tau_{P_{n,\sigma}}(x)=\sigma h_n
\quad\text{for }x\in B_n .
\]
The two laws agree everywhere except on the treated active cell. The constants satisfy
\[
8C_BC_Q<\log 5 .
\]

bbd392baf7ea4fa125ab76def78690affbd37104.title: Welfare regret identity
bbd392baf7ea4fa125ab76def78690affbd37104.body:
Under Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:bounded-outcome}, for every deterministic policy \(\pi\in\Pi\),
\[
R_P(\pi)
=
E_P\!\left[
|\tau_P(X)|\,\mathbf 1\{\pi(X)\ne\pi^\star_P(X)\}
\right]
=
\int_{\mathcal X}
|\tau_P(x)|\,\mathbf 1\{\pi(x)\ne\pi^\star_P(x)\}\,dP_X(x).
\]

702f38a4fed297578aed9b6fb857b0b65d81e5d2.title: Margin localization bound
702f38a4fed297578aed9b6fb857b0b65d81e5d2.body:
Under Assumptions~\ref{obj:ass:margin}, \ref{obj:ass:zero-effect}, and \ref{obj:ass:bounded-outcome}, and with \(D_\pi\) as in Definition~\ref{obj:def:disagreement}, there exists a constant
\[
C=C(C_m,u_0,\alpha)
\]
such that, for every \(\pi\in\Pi\),
\[
P_X(D_\pi)
\le
C\,R_P(\pi)^{\alpha/(1+\alpha)} .
\]

677a418ba53b547403fe4fb53f8cebc112e8073e.title: Overlap envelope calibration
677a418ba53b547403fe4fb53f8cebc112e8073e.body:
For \(\alpha\ge0\), \(\gamma>0\), \(h\in(0,1)\), and \(\beta\ge0\), consider the tight window
\[
v=h^\beta,
\qquad
u=h^{\beta/\gamma}.
\]
Then
\[
u^\alpha v^{1/\gamma}
=
h^{(\alpha+1)\beta/\gamma},
\]
and
\[
u^\alpha v^{1/\gamma}\ge h^\alpha
\quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad
\beta\le \frac{\alpha\gamma}{\alpha+1},
\]
with equality at \(\beta=\beta_{\alpha,\gamma}\). Hence a block with mass of order \(h^\alpha\), contrast of order \(h\), and propensity of order \(h^\beta\) satisfies the overlap-decay envelope if and only if
\[
\beta\le\beta_{\alpha,\gamma}.
\]
The exponent \(\beta_{\alpha,\gamma}\) is therefore the least informative admissible weak-arm exponent.

889e7cba0fb57f4256d134f860bff2b4cf283180.title: Witness-law membership
889e7cba0fb57f4256d134f860bff2b4cf283180.body:
Under the margin-window normalization in Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:margin-window}, for all sufficiently large \(n\), the two witness laws \(P_{n,+}\) and \(P_{n,-}\) of Definition~\ref{obj:def:two-point-witness} satisfy the observed-law membership conditions defining the class \(\mathcal P_{\alpha,\gamma}\) in Definition~\ref{obj:def:law-class}. Hence
\[
P_{n,+},P_{n,-}\in \mathcal P_{\alpha,\gamma}.
\]
Moreover, the two corresponding witness-optimal policies are
\[
x\mapsto 1
\qquad\text{and}\qquad
x\mapsto \mathbf 1\{x\notin B_n\}.
\]
If these two policies belong to \(\Pi\), then they are available as the two policy actions in the associated two-point lower-bound reduction. The normalization is \(u_0<2\), so, under the outcome bound \(|Y|\le 1\), an off-block contrast \(\tau_0\in(u_0,2)\) exists.

1c6021673d7bb4afabe2dc70a2c55fade926fcc6.title: Two-point divergence
1c6021673d7bb4afabe2dc70a2c55fade926fcc6.body:
For the witness laws in Definition~\ref{obj:def:two-point-witness}, under Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:margin-window}, the one-observation chi-square divergence satisfies
\[
\chi^2(P_{n,+}\,\|\,P_{n,-})
 \le C\,m_n q_n h_n^2,
\]
where
\[
m_n=P_X(B_n)\asymp h_n^\alpha,
\qquad
q_n\asymp h_n^{\beta_{\alpha,\gamma}}.
\]
Consequently,
\[
\chi^2(P_{n,+}\,\|\,P_{n,-})
 \lesssim h_n^{2+\alpha+\beta_{\alpha,\gamma}}.
\]
For
\[
h_n=n^{-1/(2+\alpha+\beta_{\alpha,\gamma})},
\]
the product divergence is bounded:
\[
\chi^2(P_{n,+}^{\otimes n}\,\|\,P_{n,-}^{\otimes n})\le C.
\]
Equivalently,
\[
1+\chi^2(P_{n,+}^{\otimes n}\,\|\,P_{n,-}^{\otimes n})
=
\left(1+O\!\left(h_n^{2+\alpha+\beta_{\alpha,\gamma}}\right)\right)^n
\le e^C .
\]

7402e1c92ee32843cd2ff9537d72fadcb2f54454.title: Regret separation
7402e1c92ee32843cd2ff9537d72fadcb2f54454.body:
For the witness laws in Definition~\ref{obj:def:two-point-witness}, under Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:margin-window},
\[
\inf_{\pi\in\Pi}\max_{\sigma\in\{+,-\}}
R_{P_{n,\sigma}}(\pi)
\ge
c\,h_n^{1+\alpha}.
\]
The two optimal policies disagree on \(B_n\). Therefore every \(\pi\in\Pi\) misclassifies \(B_n\) under at least one of the two laws, and under that law incurs regret at least
\[
\frac12\,P_X(B_n)\,h_n
\asymp
h_n^{1+\alpha}.
\]

59fedab6bec65c81ba0e8d8682534a41acf49328.title: Chi-square testing bound
59fedab6bec65c81ba0e8d8682534a41acf49328.body:
If
\[
\chi^2(P_+^{\otimes n}\,\|\,P_-^{\otimes n})\le C_\chi,
\]
then every test \(\psi\) taking values in \(\{+,-\}\) satisfies
\[
P_+^{\otimes n}(\psi\ne +)
+
P_-^{\otimes n}(\psi\ne -)
\ge
c(C_\chi)>0.
\]
In particular, this conclusion holds whenever the one-observation chi-square divergence is bounded by \(c_0/n\), since
\[
1+\chi^2(P_+^{\otimes n}\,\|\,P_-^{\otimes n})
=
\left(1+\chi^2(P_+\,\|\,P_-)\right)^n
\le
\left(1+\frac{c_0}{n}\right)^n
\le e^{c_0}.
\]

498cd9c743fada8085227189c34a8b6aa88d1787.title: Minimax lower bound
498cd9c743fada8085227189c34a8b6aa88d1787.body:
Under Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:margin-window}, for the minimax regret \(M_n(\alpha,\gamma)\) of Definition~\ref{obj:def:minimax-regret} over the law class \(\mathcal P_{\alpha,\gamma}\) of Definition~\ref{obj:def:law-class}, there is a constant \(c>0\), independent of \(n\), such that for all sufficiently large \(n\),
\[
M_n(\alpha,\gamma)
=
\inf_{\widehat\pi}
\sup_{P\in\mathcal P_{\alpha,\gamma}}
\mathbb E_P R_P(\widehat\pi)
\ge
c\,n^{-r_\star(\alpha,\gamma)}.
\]

fbb90889ab0062b2dd30938a32e981fa27cc5614.title: Feasible upper exponent
fbb90889ab0062b2dd30938a32e981fa27cc5614.body:
Fix a nuisance-rate regime \((a,c,C_\mu,C_{\mathrm{prod}})\) satisfying Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:polynomial-nuisance-exponents}. Over the law class \(\mathcal P_{\alpha,\gamma}\) of Definition~\ref{obj:def:law-class}, the conditional upper risk of the plain cross-fitted clipped-AIPW \(1/n\)-ERM with the deterministic schedules \(q_n\) and \(u_n\) selected for this regime satisfies the honest upper exponent
\[
U_n(\alpha,\gamma,a,c;\widehat\eta)
\lesssim
n^{-r_{\mathrm{up}}}
\]
on the stated side-condition domain, where \(r_{\mathrm{up}}=r_{\mathrm{feas}}\). The exponent is obtained for the clipped-score envelope with inverse-propensity weights bounded by \(1/q_n\), hence second-moment scale \(q_n^{-2}\), together with the deterministic clipped-score drift bound under the margin, localization, and one-sided overlap-decay conditions defining \(\mathcal P_{\alpha,\gamma}\).

d61dfa0a60ce435e839f75ccdba3541ed1c0c060.title: Minimax Lower Rate
d61dfa0a60ce435e839f75ccdba3541ed1c0c060.body:
Under Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:margin-window} and for the minimax regret over the law class in Definitions~\ref{obj:def:minimax-regret} and~\ref{obj:def:law-class}, there exists a constant \(c>0\) such that, for all sufficiently large \(n\),
\[
M_n(\alpha,\gamma)\ge c\,n^{-r_\star(\alpha,\gamma)},
\qquad
r_\star(\alpha,\gamma)
=
\frac{1+\alpha}{2+\alpha+\beta_{\alpha,\gamma}} .
\]

49b852d01701de6cfcd4deb81208526c15add430.title: Feasible Tightness Question
49b852d01701de6cfcd4deb81208526c15add430.body: In the strict-gap branch of the conditional feasible achievability bound for the regime-indexed risk \(U_n\), it is open whether a genuinely feasible estimator using estimated cross-fitted nuisance functions can attain the converse exponent \(r_\star(\alpha,\gamma)\), or whether weak-arm nuisance learning imposes the slower conditional exponent derived by the feasible upper bound.

6b20c2bf5cd3b891ccd8562af84abf0f0b84a6be.title: Clipped Score Drift
6b20c2bf5cd3b891ccd8562af84abf0f0b84a6be.body:
Under Assumptions~\ref{obj:ass:bounded-outcome}, \ref{obj:ass:positivity}, and~\ref{obj:ass:bounded-crossfit-nuisances}, and with the clipped propensity and clipped AIPW score of Definitions~\ref{obj:def:clipped-propensity} and~\ref{obj:def:clipped-aipw-score}, let \(\bar\eta=(\bar\mu_0,\bar\mu_1,\bar e)\) be any measurable plug-in nuisance triple. Define
\[
\bar e_q(x)=\min\{1-q,\max\{q,\bar e(x)\}\},
\qquad
\Delta_a(x)=\bar\mu_a(x)-\mu_a(x).
\]
Then the conditional mean drift satisfies
\[
\mathbb E_P[\Gamma_q(O;\bar\eta)\mid X=x]-\tau_P(x)
=
(\bar e_q(x)-e_P(x))
\left\{
\frac{\Delta_1(x)}{\bar e_q(x)}
+
\frac{\Delta_0(x)}{1-\bar e_q(x)}
\right\}.
\]
Consequently, the drift cancels only at covariate values where \(\bar e_q(x)=e_P(x)\) or where \(\Delta_0(x)=\Delta_1(x)=0\); cancellation does not follow merely from \(p_P(x)>q\). Under the frozen assumptions alone, no additive clipped-region bound of the asserted form is derivable.

c68c4645e2a21d22e699f6636ca3f9af8986dc6b.title: Feasible ERM Inequality
c68c4645e2a21d22e699f6636ca3f9af8986dc6b.body:
Under Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:policy-class}, Definition~\ref{obj:def:feasible-erm} defines a measurable \(\Pi\)-valued estimator \(\widehat\pi_n\) satisfying
\[
\widehat V_{n,q}(\widehat\pi_n)
\ge
\sup_{\pi\in\Pi}\widehat V_{n,q}(\pi)-\frac1n .
\]
Hence, for every comparator \(\pi^b\in\Pi\),
\[
P_n\!\left[(\widehat\pi_n-\pi^b)\Gamma_q\right]\ge -\frac1n .
\]
Under Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:optimal-in-class}, this conclusion applies with \(\pi^b=\pi^\star_P\).

25767eccb112cfff4880a0d161588152a8701b73.title: Localized Self Bound
25767eccb112cfff4880a0d161588152a8701b73.body:
Let
\[
\rho_n=\left(\frac{B^2}{n}\right)^{A_\alpha}(\log n)^p,
\qquad B\ge 1.
\]
For any centered \(\Pi\)-indexed process \(z_\pi\) whose offset positive part is controlled by the cross-fit localized offset bound, every \(\Pi\)-indexed estimator \(\widetilde\pi\) satisfying
\[
R_P(\widetilde\pi)\le 2|z_{\widetilde\pi}|+\delta_n
\]
also satisfies
\[
\mathbb E_P R_P(\widetilde\pi)\le C\{\rho_n+\delta_n\}.
\]
If \(\delta_n\le 1/n\), then the \(\delta_n\) term is absorbed into \(\rho_n\).

a854b432a661c07228a20d5cf6f1a67429ba3270.title: Localized VC Process
a854b432a661c07228a20d5cf6f1a67429ba3270.body:
Under Assumptions~\ref{obj:ass:policy-class}, \ref{obj:ass:margin}, \ref{obj:ass:zero-effect}, \ref{obj:ass:bounded-outcome}, and~\ref{obj:ass:vc-localized-envelope}, and using the regret risk and disagreement sets in Definition~\ref{obj:def:upper-risk} and Definition~\ref{obj:def:disagreement}, let
\[
\kappa=\frac{\alpha}{2+2\alpha}.
\]
For an i.i.d. sample of size \(m\), define
\[
Z_m(r)
=
\sup_{\pi\in\Pi:\,R_P(\pi)\le r}
\left|(P_m-P)g_\pi\right|,
\]
where the centered increments \(g_\pi\) have envelope \(B\) and satisfy
\[
\mathbb E_P[g_\pi^2\mid X]\le C B^2 P_X(D_\pi).
\]
Then, for every regret radius \(r\),
\[
\mathbb E_P Z_m(r)
\le
C B\,m^{-1/2} r^\kappa(\log m)^p .
\]

c08761e1579546cc9063fa5fdc12bdcbffdfe940.title: Cross-Fit Localized Reduction
c08761e1579546cc9063fa5fdc12bdcbffdfe940.body:
Let \(Z_{\mathrm{cf}}(r)\) denote the pooled cross-fit localized supremum formed by averaging centered evaluation-fold increments.

Assume:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Data and folds.)} The observations are i.i.d. as in Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:iid}, the fixed balanced cross-fitting scheme of Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:fixed-crossfit-fold-count} is used, and the risk is evaluated in the setting of Definition~\ref{obj:def:upper-risk}.
\item \textbf{(Policies and localization.)} The policy class satisfies Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:policy-class}, the margin and zero-effect conditions of Assumptions~\ref{obj:ass:margin} and~\ref{obj:ass:zero-effect} hold, and disagreements are measured by \(D_\pi\) as in Definition~\ref{obj:def:disagreement}.
\item \textbf{(Outcome and envelope.)} The bounded-outcome condition of Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:bounded-outcome} and the localized VC envelope condition of Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:vc-localized-envelope} hold.
\item \textbf{(Foldwise increments.)} Conditional on the training folds, the centered foldwise increments have envelope \(B\) and conditional second moment bounded by
\[
C B^2 P_X(D_\pi).
\]
\end{itemize}
Then, conditional on the training folds, each balanced evaluation fold is i.i.d., and for every \(r\),
\[
E_P\!\left[ Z_{\mathrm{cf}}(r)\mid \text{training folds}\right]
\le
C B n^{-1/2} r^{\alpha/(2+2\alpha)}(\log n)^p .
\]
Consequently,
\[
E_P Z_{\mathrm{cf}}(r)
\le
C B n^{-1/2} r^{\alpha/(2+2\alpha)}(\log n)^p .
\]

eb527e0f1ba78a699f9a81da2d0be172806d1904.title: Cross-Fit Offset Control
eb527e0f1ba78a699f9a81da2d0be172806d1904.body:
Let \(G_{\mathrm{cf}}(\pi)\) be the pooled cross-fit centered process indexed by \(\pi\in\Pi\).

Assume:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Data and folds.)} The observations are i.i.d. as in Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:iid}, and the fixed balanced cross-fitting scheme of Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:fixed-crossfit-fold-count} is used.
\item \textbf{(Policies and localization.)} The policy class satisfies Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:policy-class}, the margin and zero-effect conditions of Assumptions~\ref{obj:ass:margin} and~\ref{obj:ass:zero-effect} hold, and \(D_\pi\) is the disagreement set in Definition~\ref{obj:def:disagreement}.
\item \textbf{(Outcome.)} The bounded-outcome condition of Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:bounded-outcome} holds.
\item \textbf{(Foldwise increments.)} The foldwise centered increments have envelope \(B\) and conditional second moment bounded by
\[
C B^2 P_X(D_\pi).
\]
\end{itemize}
Then
\[
E_P \sup_{\pi\in\Pi}
\left\{2\lvert G_{\mathrm{cf}}(\pi)\rvert-\frac{R_P(\pi)}{4}\right\}_{+}
\le
C\left(\frac{B^2}{n}\right)^{A_\alpha}(\log n)^p .
\]

11dedd7ad9b58ea20a51a59ca9ba94b715636b55.title: Clipped-Score Envelope
11dedd7ad9b58ea20a51a59ca9ba94b715636b55.body:
Under the bounded-outcome condition of Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:bounded-outcome}, for every \(q\in(0,1/2]\) and every cross-fitted nuisance triple \(\widehat\eta^{(-k)}\), the clipped AIPW score of Definition~\ref{obj:def:clipped-aipw-score} satisfies
\[
\left|\Gamma_q\!\left(O;\widehat\eta^{(-k)}\right)\right|\le \frac{C}{q}
\quad\text{almost surely}.
\]
Consequently,
\[
E\!\left[\Gamma_q\!\left(O;\widehat\eta^{(-k)}\right)^2\mid X\right]
\le \frac{C}{q^2}.
\]

8fef7f2596324078d72f0621971d2e319a8ad84b.title: Clipped-Region Localization
8fef7f2596324078d72f0621971d2e319a8ad84b.body:
Assume \(\gamma>0\). Under the overlap-decay and zero-effect conditions of Assumptions~\ref{obj:ass:overlap-decay} and~\ref{obj:ass:zero-effect}, together with the bounded-outcome condition of Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:bounded-outcome}, every policy \(\pi\) with regret \(r=R_P(\pi)\) satisfies, for its disagreement set \(D_\pi\) from Definition~\ref{obj:def:disagreement},
\[
P_X\!\left(D_\pi\cap\{x:p_P(x)\le q\}\right)
\le
C u^\alpha q^{1/\gamma}+\frac{r}{u}
\]
whenever \(0<u\le u_0\) and \(q\le c_o u^\gamma\).

4f01ff2c57010b05fb6052b9eef49d96b22934c7.title: Clipping Balance Exponent
4f01ff2c57010b05fb6052b9eef49d96b22934c7.body:
Under the polynomial nuisance-rate exponents of Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:polynomial-nuisance-exponents}, the deterministic clipping and localization schedules satisfy the following exponent balance.

For \(\gamma>0\), let
\[
A_\alpha=\frac{1+\alpha}{2+\alpha},
\qquad
q_n=q_0 n^{-s_{\mathrm{feas}}},
\qquad
u_n=\bar u\, n^{-t_{\mathrm{feas}}},
\]
and
\[
g_{\mathrm{joint}}
=
\max_{\substack{0\le s\le 1/2\\ 0\le t\le s/\gamma}}
\min\left\{
A_\alpha(1-2s),\,
c-s,\,
a+\frac{s}{2\gamma}+\frac{\alpha t}{2},\,
2a-t
\right\}.
\]
Then the master bound is at most
\[
C\left\{n^{-r_\star(\alpha,\gamma)}+n^{-g_{\mathrm{joint}}}\right\}(\log n)^p
=
C n^{-\min\{r_\star(\alpha,\gamma),g_{\mathrm{joint}}\}}(\log n)^p .
\]
Equivalently, the optimized upper-bound exponent is
\[
r_{\mathrm{up}}=r_{\mathrm{feas}}
=
\min\{r_\star(\alpha,\gamma),g_{\mathrm{joint}}\}.
\]

For \(\gamma=0\), the corresponding exponent is
\[
\min\{A_\alpha,c\}.
\]

9c07c003cf8d66ca5ecb3b8bb062bf5ddc0bf0fd.title: Crude Localized Master Bound
9c07c003cf8d66ca5ecb3b8bb062bf5ddc0bf0fd.body:
Assume:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Law class and welfare.)} The law \(P\) belongs to the class in Definition~\ref{obj:def:law-class}, and regret \(R_P(\pi)\) and welfare \(V_P(\pi)\) are as in Definition~\ref{obj:def:welfare-regret}.
\item \textbf{(Policies and cross-fitting.)} The policy class satisfies Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:policy-class}, and the fixed fold structure satisfies Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:fixed-crossfit-fold-count}.
\item \textbf{(Nuisance rates.)} The nuisance estimators satisfy Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:nuisance-rate} with rates \(r_{\mu,n}\) and \(r_{e,n}\), and the polynomial nuisance exponents satisfy Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:polynomial-nuisance-exponents}.
\item \textbf{(Basic inequality and bias.)} The feasible ERM basic inequality of Lemma~\ref{obj:lem:feasible-erm-basic-inequality} and the clipped-score bias bound of Lemma~\ref{obj:lem:clip-bias} hold.
\item \textbf{(Empirical process control.)} The localized cross-fit offset bound of Lemma~\ref{obj:lem:crossfit-localized-offset-control} and the localized VC self-bound of Lemma~\ref{obj:lem:localized-vc-self-bound} hold.
\end{itemize}
Then the cross-fitted clipped-AIPW \(1/n\)-ERM \(\widehat\pi_n\) satisfies the following bounds.

If \(\gamma>0\) and \(q\le c_o u^\gamma\), then
\[
E_P R_P(\widehat\pi_n)
\le
C\left\{
n^{-r_\star(\alpha,\gamma)}
+
(nq^2)^{-A_\alpha}
+
\frac{r_{\mu,n}r_{e,n}}{q}
+
r_{\mu,n}u^{\alpha/2}q^{1/(2\gamma)}
+
\frac{r_{\mu,n}^2}{u}
\right\}(\log n)^p .
\]

If \(\gamma=0\) and \(q\le \underline p/2\) is fixed, then
\[
E_P R_P(\widehat\pi_n)
\le
C\left\{n^{-A_\alpha}+r_{\mu,n}r_{e,n}\right\}(\log n)^p .
\]

ac08cd3dcf891710f7e27328438936e4c847fbc3.title: Localized Clipped Drift Bound
ac08cd3dcf891710f7e27328438936e4c847fbc3.body:
Under Assumptions~\ref{obj:ass:nuisance-rate}, \ref{obj:ass:polynomial-nuisance-exponents}, \ref{obj:ass:overlap-decay}, \ref{obj:ass:zero-effect}, \ref{obj:ass:bounded-outcome}, and \ref{obj:ass:strict-overlap-endpoint}, and Lemma~\ref{obj:lem:clipped-region-localization}, define
\[
b_q(x)
=
\mathbb E_P\!\left[\Gamma_q(O;\widehat\eta^{(-k)})\mid X=x\right]
-
\tau_P(x),
\]
with the cross-fitted nuisance estimators fixed on the evaluation fold.

If \(\gamma>0\), then there is a finite constant \(C\) such that every policy \(\pi\) with \(r=R_P(\pi)\) satisfies
\[
\left|P\!\left[(\pi-\pi^\star_P)b_q\right]\right|
\le
C\left\{
\frac{r_{\mu,n}r_{e,n}}{q}
+
r_{\mu,n}u^{\alpha/2}q^{1/(2\gamma)}
+
r_{\mu,n}\left(\frac{r}{u}\right)^{1/2}
\right\}
\]
whenever \(q\le c_o u^\gamma\).

If \(\gamma=0\) and \(q\le \underline p/2\) is fixed, then
\[
\left|P\!\left[(\pi-\pi^\star_P)b_q\right]\right|
\le
C r_{\mu,n}r_{e,n}.
\]

8a86a6cec0537460553f3683e128836d5e9dba6a.title: Conditional Feasible Upper Bound
8a86a6cec0537460553f3683e128836d5e9dba6a.body:
Fix a nuisance-rate regime \((a,c,C_\mu,C_{\mathrm{prod}})\) satisfying Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:polynomial-nuisance-exponents}. Let \(\widehat\pi_n\) be the cross-fitted clipped-AIPW \(1/n\)-ERM of Definition~\ref{obj:def:feasible-erm}, formed over the policy class of Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:policy-class} with the fixed finite cross-fitting scheme of Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:fixed-crossfit-fold-count} and bounded cross-fitted nuisances as in Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:bounded-crossfit-nuisances}. For the observed-law class \(\mathcal P(\alpha,\gamma)\) of Definition~\ref{obj:def:law-class}, and for the conditional worst-case risk \(U_n(\alpha,\gamma,a,c;\widehat\eta)\) of Definition~\ref{obj:def:upper-risk} over its stated side-condition domain, the honest conditional feasible upper exponent for this procedure is \(r_{\mathrm{up}}\). Equivalently, for a finite constant \(C\),
\[
U_n(\alpha,\gamma,a,c;\widehat\eta)
\le
C n^{-r_{\mathrm{up}}}(\log n)^p
\]
on that side-condition domain.

52e5a781633eb72b7a0183289f9126fb03fe215d.title: Crude Localized Master Bound

fb6c6e66373115aac9823371e3c6718375015025.title: Feasible Upper Exponent
fb6c6e66373115aac9823371e3c6718375015025.body:
Fix a nuisance regime \((a,c,C_\mu,C_{\mathrm{prod}})\) satisfying Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:polynomial-nuisance-exponents}. Consider the law class \(\mathcal P_{\alpha,\gamma}\) of Definition~\ref{obj:def:law-class}, the feasible cross-fitted clipped-AIPW \(1/n\)-ERM \(\widehat\pi_n\) of Definition~\ref{obj:def:feasible-erm}, and the conditional upper risk \(U_n(\alpha,\gamma,a,c;\widehat\eta)\) of Definition~\ref{obj:def:upper-risk}.

Assume:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Policy class.)} The policy class satisfies Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:policy-class}.
\item \textbf{(Cross-fitting.)} The number of folds satisfies Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:fixed-crossfit-fold-count}.
\item \textbf{(Nuisance boundedness.)} The cross-fitted nuisance estimators satisfy Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:bounded-crossfit-nuisances}.
\item \textbf{(Localized envelopes.)} The localized empirical-process and offset envelope conditions in Assumptions~\ref{obj:ass:vc-localized-envelope} and~\ref{obj:ass:vc-localized-offset-envelope} hold.
\end{itemize}

Then the conditional upper risk of \(\widehat\pi_n\), uniformly over \(\mathcal P_{\alpha,\gamma}\) and over the side-condition domain in Definition~\ref{obj:def:upper-risk}, has upper exponent \(r_{\mathrm{up}}\):
\[
U_n(\alpha,\gamma,a,c;\widehat\eta)\le C n^{-r_{\mathrm{up}}}(\log n)^p .
\]

174fa109ea314eaa0ea2fbbc0dff3e74c529db46.title: Cross-Fit Offset Control
174fa109ea314eaa0ea2fbbc0dff3e74c529db46.body:
Let \(G_{\mathrm{cf}}(\pi)\) be the pooled cross-fitted centered policy-indexed process. Suppose that, conditionally on the nuisance fits, the foldwise centered increments have envelope \(B\) and conditional second moment bounded by
\[
C B^2 P_X(D_\pi),
\]
where \(D_\pi\) is the disagreement set of Definition~\ref{obj:def:disagreement}.

Assume:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Sampling.)} The observations are i.i.d. as in Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:iid}.
\item \textbf{(Bounded outcomes.)} The outcome is bounded as in Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:bounded-outcome}.
\item \textbf{(Policy class.)} The policy class satisfies Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:policy-class}.
\item \textbf{(Cross-fitting.)} The number of folds satisfies Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:fixed-crossfit-fold-count}.
\item \textbf{(Margin and zero effect.)} The margin and zero-effect conditions in Assumptions~\ref{obj:ass:margin} and~\ref{obj:ass:zero-effect} hold.
\item \textbf{(Offset envelope.)} The localized offset envelope condition in Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:vc-localized-offset-envelope} holds.
\end{itemize}

Then
\[
\mathbb E_P
\left[
\sup_{\pi\in\Pi}
\left\{
2|G_{\mathrm{cf}}(\pi)|-\frac{R_P(\pi)}{4}
\right\}_{+}
\right]
\le
C\left(\frac{B^2}{n}\right)^{A_\alpha}(\log n)^p .
\]

9f1d0a46e61a979bc48858e4359922dd1879e54e.title: Crude Localized Master Bound
9f1d0a46e61a979bc48858e4359922dd1879e54e.body:
Let \(\widehat\pi_n\) be the cross-fitted clipped-AIPW \(1/n\)-ERM of Definition~\ref{obj:def:feasible-erm}. Suppose \(P\in\mathcal P_{\alpha,\gamma}\), where \(\mathcal P_{\alpha,\gamma}\) is the law class of Definition~\ref{obj:def:law-class}.

Assume:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Policy and oracle.)} The policy class and oracle satisfy Assumptions~\ref{obj:ass:policy-class} and~\ref{obj:ass:optimal-in-class}; welfare and regret are as in Definition~\ref{obj:def:welfare-regret}.
\item \textbf{(Sampling.)} The observations are i.i.d. as in Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:iid}.
\item \textbf{(Nuisance rates.)} The nuisance estimators satisfy Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:nuisance-rate} and the polynomial nuisance exponent condition in Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:polynomial-nuisance-exponents}.
\item \textbf{(Cross-fitting and boundedness.)} The number of folds is fixed as in Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:fixed-crossfit-fold-count}, and the cross-fitted nuisance estimators satisfy Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:bounded-crossfit-nuisances}.
\item \textbf{(Localized empirical processes.)} The localized envelope and offset envelope conditions in Assumptions~\ref{obj:ass:vc-localized-envelope} and~\ref{obj:ass:vc-localized-offset-envelope} hold.
\item \textbf{(Basic inequality, bias, and process bounds.)} The feasible ERM basic inequality of Lemma~\ref{obj:lem:feasible-erm-basic-inequality}, the clipped-bias identity of Lemma~\ref{obj:lem:clip-bias}, the cross-fit offset bound of Lemma~\ref{obj:lem:crossfit-localized-offset-control}, and the localized VC self-bound of Lemma~\ref{obj:lem:localized-vc-self-bound} hold.
\end{itemize}

If \(\gamma>0\) and
\[
q\le c_o u^\gamma,
\]
then
\[
\mathbb E_P R_P(\widehat\pi_n)
\le
C\left\{
n^{-r_\star}
+(nq^2)^{-A_\alpha}
+\frac{r_{\mu,n}r_{e,n}}{q}
+r_{\mu,n}u^{\alpha/2}q^{1/(2\gamma)}
+\frac{r_{\mu,n}^2}{u}
\right\}(\log n)^p .
\]

If \(\gamma=0\) and \(q\le \underline p/2\) is fixed, then
\[
\mathbb E_P R_P(\widehat\pi_n)
\le
C\left\{
n^{-A_\alpha}+r_{\mu,n}r_{e,n}
\right\}(\log n)^p .
\]

3ed4c130dfc6405ab0e3603d08b94abf7f251a35.title: Margin Localization
3ed4c130dfc6405ab0e3603d08b94abf7f251a35.body:
Suppose that the following conditions hold:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{Margin condition.} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:margin} holds.
\item \textbf{Zero-effect convention.} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:zero-effect} holds.
\item \textbf{Bounded outcomes.} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:bounded-outcome} holds.
\item \textbf{Disagreement sets.} \(D_\pi\) is defined as in Definition~\ref{obj:def:disagreement}.
\end{itemize}
Then there exists a constant
\[
C=C(C_m,u_0,\alpha)
\]
such that, for every \(\pi\in\Pi\),
\[
P_X(D_\pi)
\le
C\,R_P(\pi)^{\alpha/(1+\alpha)} .
\]

2f63cca3145e6e7f9fe8b755d56b5e6b70f27392.title: Localized VC Process Bound
2f63cca3145e6e7f9fe8b755d56b5e6b70f27392.body:
Suppose that the following conditions hold:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{Policy class.} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:policy-class} holds.
\item \textbf{Margin condition.} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:margin} holds.
\item \textbf{Zero-effect convention.} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:zero-effect} holds.
\item \textbf{Bounded outcomes.} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:bounded-outcome} holds.
\item \textbf{Localized VC envelope.} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:vc-localized-envelope} holds.
\item \textbf{Upper-risk notation.} Regret radii are interpreted using Definition~\ref{obj:def:upper-risk}.
\item \textbf{Disagreement sets.} \(D_\pi\) is defined as in Definition~\ref{obj:def:disagreement}.
\end{itemize}
Let
\[
\kappa=\frac{\alpha}{2+2\alpha}.
\]
For an i.i.d. sample of size \(m\), define
\[
Z_m(r)
=
\sup_{\pi\in\Pi:\,R_P(\pi)\le r}
\left|(P_m-P)g_\pi\right|,
\]
where the centered increments \(g_\pi\) have envelope \(B\) and satisfy
\[
\mathbb E_P[g_\pi^2\mid X]\le C B^2 P_X(D_\pi).
\]
Then, for every regret radius \(r\),
\[
\mathbb E_P Z_m(r)
\le
C B\,m^{-1/2} r^\kappa(\log m)^p .
\]

f9ba2d1bb4530b741c54def9bd1165d821ba738c.title: Clipped Region Localization
f9ba2d1bb4530b741c54def9bd1165d821ba738c.body:
Suppose that the following conditions hold:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{Positive weak-overlap exponent.} \(\gamma>0\).
\item \textbf{Overlap decay.} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:overlap-decay} holds.
\item \textbf{Zero-effect convention.} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:zero-effect} holds.
\item \textbf{Bounded outcomes.} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:bounded-outcome} holds.
\item \textbf{Disagreement sets.} \(D_\pi\) is defined as in Definition~\ref{obj:def:disagreement}.
\end{itemize}
Then every policy \(\pi\) with regret \(r=R_P(\pi)\) satisfies
\[
P_X\!\left(D_\pi\cap\{x:p_P(x)\le q\}\right)
\le
C u^\alpha q^{1/\gamma}+\frac{r}{u}
\]
whenever \(0<u\le u_0\) and \(q\le c_o u^\gamma\).

29a8d14beb220e8287da87d5f6ea27af6eb09cf4.title: Localized Clipped Drift
29a8d14beb220e8287da87d5f6ea27af6eb09cf4.body:
Suppose that the following conditions hold:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{Nuisance rates.} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:nuisance-rate} holds.
\item \textbf{Polynomial nuisance exponents.} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:polynomial-nuisance-exponents} holds.
\item \textbf{Clipped-region localization.} Lemma~\ref{obj:lem:clipped-region-localization} holds.
\item \textbf{Overlap decay.} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:overlap-decay} holds.
\item \textbf{Zero-effect convention.} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:zero-effect} holds.
\item \textbf{Bounded outcomes.} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:bounded-outcome} holds.
\item \textbf{Strict-overlap endpoint.} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:strict-overlap-endpoint} holds.
\end{itemize}
Define
\[
b_q(x)
=
\mathbb E_P\!\left[\Gamma_q(O;\widehat\eta^{(-k)})\mid X=x\right]
-
\tau_P(x),
\]
with the cross-fitted nuisance estimators fixed on the evaluation fold.

If \(\gamma>0\), then there is a finite constant \(C\) such that every policy \(\pi\) with \(r=R_P(\pi)\) satisfies
\[
\left|P\!\left[(\pi-\pi^\star_P)b_q\right]\right|
\le
C\left\{
\frac{r_{\mu,n}r_{e,n}}{q}
+
r_{\mu,n}u^{\alpha/2}q^{1/(2\gamma)}
+
r_{\mu,n}\left(\frac{r}{u}\right)^{1/2}
\right\}
\]
whenever \(q\le c_o u^\gamma\).

If \(\gamma=0\) and \(q\le \underline p/2\) is fixed, then
\[
\left|P\!\left[(\pi-\pi^\star_P)b_q\right]\right|
\le
C r_{\mu,n}r_{e,n}.
\]

85bc53069c126e152cb2c45096514b4c69e78445.title: Margin Localization Bound
85bc53069c126e152cb2c45096514b4c69e78445.body:
For fixed margin parameters $C_m,\alpha,u_0$, there exists a constant
$C=C(C_m,u_0,\alpha)>0$ such that the following holds for every observed law $P$ and every policy class $\Pi$.

Assume:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Margin.)} $P$ satisfies Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:margin} with parameters $C_m,\alpha,u_0$.
\item \textbf{(Zero effect.)} $P$ and $\Pi$ satisfy Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:zero-effect}.
\item \textbf{(Observed-law setup.)} $P$ is a well-formed observed law: $P_X$ is the covariate marginal of the observed data law, $\tau_P=\mu_1-\mu_0$, and the propensity and regression functions have their usual conditional-mean interpretation.
\item \textbf{(Bounded outcomes.)} $P$ satisfies Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:bounded-outcome}.
\item \textbf{(Policy measurability.)} Every $\pi\in\Pi$ is measurable.
\end{itemize}
Then, for every $\pi\in\Pi$, with $D_\pi$ as in Definition~\ref{obj:def:disagreement},
\[
P_X(D_\pi)
\le
C\, R_P(\pi)^{\alpha/(1+\alpha)} .
\]

5efe9667dfc3e99850f973549e60b0da77a07aa8.title: Feasible Upper Risk Bound
5efe9667dfc3e99850f973549e60b0da77a07aa8.body:
Fix a fold count \(K\), a deterministic fold assignment \(k(i)\in\{1,\ldots,K\}\), a policy class \(\Pi\), an enumeration \(\Pi_0=\{\pi_j:j\ge 1\}\), cross-fitted nuisance estimates \(\widehat\eta^{(-k)}=(\widehat\mu^{(-k)}_0,\widehat\mu^{(-k)}_1,\widehat e^{(-k)})\), and nuisance-rate sequences \(r_{\mu,n}\) and \(r_{e,n}\). Suppose:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Exponent and rate inputs.)} \(0\le \gamma\), the nuisance exponents satisfy Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:polynomial-nuisance-exponents} with parameters \((a,c,C_\mu,C_{\mathrm{prod}})\), \(C_\mu\ge 0\), \(C_{\mathrm{prod}}\ge 0\), \(r_{\mu,n}\ge 0\) and \(r_{e,n}\ge 0\) for all sufficiently large \(n\), and the feasible-rate input conditions for \((\gamma,c_o,\underline p,u_0,q_0,\bar u)\) hold.
\item \textbf{(Law and policy side conditions.)} The law class is Definition~\ref{obj:def:law-class}; the policy class satisfies Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:policy-class} with VC dimension \(d_\Pi\); the uniform localized envelope and offset-envelope assumptions, Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:vc-localized-envelope} and Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:vc-localized-offset-envelope}, hold for \(\Pi\) and \(\alpha\).
\item \textbf{(Cross-fitting and ERM construction.)} The fold assignment satisfies Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:fixed-crossfit-fold-count}; the nuisance estimates satisfy Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:bounded-crossfit-nuisances} fold by fold; the enumeration is a pointwise dense skeleton for \(\Pi\); and the estimator is the feasible clipped-AIPW \(1/n\)-ERM of Definition~\ref{obj:def:feasible-erm}, with clipping schedule \(q_n=q_0\) when \(\gamma=0\) and \(q_n=q_0 n^{-s_{\mathrm{feas}}}\) otherwise.
\item \textbf{(Regularity.)} The nuisance estimates are measurable, their nuisance errors have the required \(L_2(P_X)\) membership uniformly over laws in Definition~\ref{obj:def:law-class} for all sufficiently large \(n\), and the Bochner integrability and bounded-supremum conditions needed to define the regret risk and the pooled cross-fit localized suprema hold for all sufficiently large \(n\).
\end{itemize}
Then there exist constants \(C>0\) and \(p\ge 0\) such that, for all sufficiently large \(n\),
\[
U_n(\alpha,\gamma,a,c;\widehat\eta)
\le
C\, n^{-r_{\mathrm{feas}}}\,(\log n)^p,
\]
where \(U_n(\alpha,\gamma,a,c;\widehat\eta)\) is the conditional upper risk of Definition~\ref{obj:def:upper-risk} for the feasible clipped-AIPW ERM above, and \(r_{\mathrm{feas}}\) is the feasible upper exponent determined by the stated feasible-rate inputs.
